{

    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.h"},

    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.h"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPParser"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::LogViolationDetails"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl", "symbol": "nsIContentSecurityPolicy"}
  ],
  "text": "The Firefox CSP enforcement infrastructure under dom/security/ consists of the following key files:\n\n1. **dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp** (and nsCSPParser.h) — Defines and implements `nsCSPParser`, the CSP directive parser that tokenizes and parses Content Security Policy strings into policy objects.\n\n2. **dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp** (and nsCSPContext.h) — Implements `nsCSPContext`, the main CSP context class that holds policies and enforces them. It implements the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface. This file also contains `GetAllowsInline()` (the method that performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts, using `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and `LogViolationDetails()` for violation reporting.\n\n3. **dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp** (and nsCSPUtils.h) — Implements CSP utility classes including `nsCSPPolicy`, `nsCSPDirective`, and related helper classes. Contains `ShouldCreateViolationForNewTrustedTypesPolicy` and other violation-related logic. Also includes a factory method for creating CSP contexts from policy strings.\n\n4. **dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl** — Declares the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface, which defines the scriptable API for modeling and enforcing CSPs, including constants for CSP directives (e.g., `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and methods like `getAllowsInline`, `getAllowsEval`, and `logViolationDetails`.\n\n5. **dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp** (and CSPEvalChecker.h) — Performs script-src evaluation specifically for `eval()` calls. Uses `aCSP->GetAllowsEval()` to determine if eval is permitted and logs violations via `LogViolationDetails` with `VIOLATION_TYPE_EVAL`. This is the primary entry point for eval()-based script-src enforcement. Inline script evaluation (non-eval) is handled via `nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline()` in nsCSPContext.cpp."
}

{
  "files": [

  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPParser"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::LogViolationDetails"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl", "symbol": "nsIContentSecurityPolicy"}
  ],
  "text": "The Firefox CSP enforcement infrastructure under dom/security/ consists of the following key files:\n\n1. dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp (and nsCSPParser.h) — Defines and implements nsCSPParser, the CSP directive parser that tokenizes and parses Content Security Policy strings into policy objects via the static method parseContentSecurityPolicy().\n\n2. dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp (and nsCSPContext.h) — Implements nsCSPContext, the main CSP context class that holds policies and enforces them. It implements the nsIContentSecurityPolicy XPCOM interface. This file contains GetAllowsInline() which performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts (using SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE), and LogViolationDetails() for violation reporting.\n\n3. dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp (and nsCSPUtils.h) — Implements CSP utility classes including nsCSPPolicy, nsCSPDirective, and related helper classes. Contains violation-related logic such as ShouldCreateViolationForNewTrustedTypesPolicy and helper methods for policy evaluation.\n\n4. dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl — Declares the nsIContentSecurityPolicy XPCOM interface, which defines the scriptable API for modeling and enforcing CSPs, including directive constants (e.g., SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE) and methods like getAllowsInline, getAllowsEval, and logViolationDetails.\n\n5. dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp (and CSPEvalChecker.h) — Performs script-src evaluation specifically for eval() calls. Uses aCSP->GetAllowsEval() to determine if eval is permitted and logs violations. This is the primary file for eval-based script-src enforcement; inline script-src enforcement is handled via nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline() in nsCSPContext.cpp."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Firefox Content Security Policy Enforcement Audit

## Your Task

Audit the Content Security Policy (CSP) enforcement infrastructure in Firefox. Find all C++ source files in `mozilla-firefox/firefox` under `dom/security/` that implement CSP parsing, evaluation, and violation reporting. Specifically: 1. The file that defines `nsCSPParser` — the CSP directive parser. 2. The file that implements `nsCSPContext` — the main CSP context that holds policies. 3. The file that implements CSP violation reporting (`nsCSPUtils` or similar). 4. The header file that declares the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface. 5. The file under `dom/security/` that performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts. Report each file path and its primary class or function.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/firefox--871325b8` (mozilla-firefox/firefox)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

Now I have enough information to write the answer. Let me compile the findings:

1. **nsCSPParser** (`nsCSPParser.cpp`) — defines and implements `nsCSPParser`, the CSP directive parser
2. **nsCSPContext** (`nsCSPContext.cpp`) — implements `nsCSPContext`, the main CSP context holding policies; also handles inline script evaluation via `GetAllowsInline`
3. **nsCSPUtils** (`nsCSPUtils.cpp`) — implements CSP utility classes like `nsCSPPolicy`, `nsCSPDirective`, and violation-related utilities
4. **nsIContentSecurityPolicy** (`dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl`) — declares the XPCOM interface
5. **CSPEvalChecker.cpp** — handles script-src eval() enforcement; but inline script-src evaluation is in `nsCSPContext.cpp` via `GetAllowsInline`

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of the findings:

## Firefox CSP Enforcement Files in `dom/security/`

| File | Primary Class/Function |
|------|----------------------|
| `dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp` | `nsCSPParser` — CSP directive parser |
| `dom/security/nsCSPParser.h` | `nsCSPParser` — parser class declaration |
| `dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp` | `nsCSPContext` — main CSP context; `GetAllowsInline()` for inline script-src evaluation; `LogViolationDetails()` for violation reporting |
| `dom/security/nsCSPContext.h` | `nsCSPContext` — class declaration |
| `dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp` | `nsCSPPolicy`, `nsCSPDirective` — policy/directive utilities and violation helpers |
| `dom/security/nsCSPUtils.h` | CSP utility class declarations |
| `dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl` | `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` — XPCOM interface declaration |
| `dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp` | `CSPEvalChecker` — script-src `eval()` enforcement |
| `dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.h` | `CSPEvalChecker` — eval checker declaration |

Key notes:
- **Inline script evaluation** (script-src for inline scripts) is handled by `nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline()` in `nsCSPContext.cpp`
- **Violation reporting** lives primarily in `nsCSPContext.cpp` (`LogViolationDetails`, `GatherSecurityPolicyViolationEventData`)
- **eval() enforcement** is in `CSPEvalChecker.cpp`

{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPUtils.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.h"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPParser"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp", "symbol": "nsCSPContext::LogViolationDetails"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl", "symbol": "nsIContentSecurityPolicy"}
  ],
  "text": "The Firefox CSP enforcement infrastructure under dom/security/ consists of the following key files:\n\n1. **dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp** (and nsCSPParser.h) — Defines and implements `nsCSPParser`, the CSP directive parser that tokenizes and parses Content Security Policy strings into policy objects.\n\n2. **dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp** (and nsCSPContext.h) — Implements `nsCSPContext`, the main CSP context class that holds policies and enforces them. It implements the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface. This file also contains `GetAllowsInline()` (the method that performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts, using `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and `LogViolationDetails()` for violation reporting.\n\n3. **dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp** (and nsCSPUtils.h) — Implements CSP utility classes including `nsCSPPolicy`, `nsCSPDirective`, and related helper classes. Contains `ShouldCreateViolationForNewTrustedTypesPolicy` and other violation-related logic. Also includes a factory method for creating CSP contexts from policy strings.\n\n4. **dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl** — Declares the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface, which defines the scriptable API for modeling and enforcing CSPs, including constants for CSP directives (e.g., `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and methods like `getAllowsInline`, `getAllowsEval`, and `logViolationDetails`.\n\n5. **dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp** (and CSPEvalChecker.h) — Performs script-src evaluation specifically for `eval()` calls. Uses `aCSP->GetAllowsEval()` to determine if eval is permitted and logs violations via `LogViolationDetails` with `VIOLATION_TYPE_EVAL`. This is the primary entry point for eval()-based script-src enforcement. Inline script evaluation (non-eval) is handled via `nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline()` in nsCSPContext.cpp."
}

  "text": "The Firefox CSP enforcement infrastructure under dom/security/ consists of the following key files:\n\n1. **dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp** (and nsCSPParser.h) — Defines and implements `nsCSPParser`, the CSP directive parser that tokenizes and parses Content Security Policy strings into policy objects.\n\n2. **dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp** (and nsCSPContext.h) — Implements `nsCSPContext`, the main CSP context class that holds policies and enforces them. It implements the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface. This file also contains `GetAllowsInline()` (the method that performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts, using `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and `LogViolationDetails()` for violation reporting.\n\n3. **dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp** (and nsCSPUtils.h) — Implements CSP utility classes including `nsCSPPolicy`, `nsCSPDirective`, and related helper classes. Contains `ShouldCreateViolationForNewTrustedTypesPolicy` and other violation-related logic. Also includes a factory method for creating CSP contexts from policy strings.\n\n4. **dom/interfaces/security/nsIContentSecurityPolicy.idl** — Declares the `nsIContentSecurityPolicy` XPCOM interface, which defines the scriptable API for modeling and enforcing CSPs, including constants for CSP directives (e.g., `SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE`) and methods like `getAllowsInline`, `getAllowsEval`, and `logViolationDetails`.\n\n5. **dom/security/CSPEvalChecker.cpp** (and CSPEvalChecker.h) — Performs script-src evaluation specifically for `eval()` calls. Uses `aCSP->GetAllowsEval()` to determine if eval is permitted and logs violations via `LogViolationDetails` with `VIOLATION_TYPE_EVAL`. This is the primary entry point for eval()-based script-src enforcement. Inline script evaluation (non-eval) is handled via `nsCSPContext::GetAllowsInline()` in nsCSPContext.cpp."

  "text": "The Firefox CSP enforcement infrastructure under dom/security/ consists of the following key files:\n\n1. dom/security/nsCSPParser.cpp (and nsCSPParser.h) — Defines and implements nsCSPParser, the CSP directive parser that tokenizes and parses Content Security Policy strings into policy objects via the static method parseContentSecurityPolicy().\n\n2. dom/security/nsCSPContext.cpp (and nsCSPContext.h) — Implements nsCSPContext, the main CSP context class that holds policies and enforces them. It implements the nsIContentSecurityPolicy XPCOM interface. This file contains GetAllowsInline() which performs script-src evaluation for inline scripts (using SCRIPT_SRC_ELEM_DIRECTIVE), and LogViolationDetails() for violation reporting.\n\n3. dom/security/nsCSPUtils.cpp (and nsCSPUtils.h) — Implements CSP utility classes including nsCSPPolicy, nsCSPDirective, and related helper classes.
